Bus Timings

67 Bus Timings Al Reem Shams to Marina Mall

September 18, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus 67 in Abu Dhabi provides essential connectivity between Al Reem Shams and Marina Mall, serving key residential, commercial, and cultural landmarks along the way. This route is integral for commuters seeking efficient transportation across various neighborhoods and destinations in the city.

Bus Timings

Bus 67 operates daily from the Al Reem Shams departure point, starting its service from Al Reem Shams as early as 03:52 and running until late at night. This schedule ensures frequent and reliable services tailored to meet a variety of schedules and travel preferences.

From Al Reem Shams From Marina Mall

04:47 04:50
05:05 05:13

Download PDF Bus 67

Bus 67 Timetable

To view the complete schedule, you can visit the official AD Mobility website, or check this link for 67 Bus Timings.

Bus Route

The Bus 67 route includes stops at the following locations:

  1. Al Reem Shams

  2. Al Reem Shams

  3. Repton School

  4. Union National Bank

  5. Gate Towers

  6. Al Zawi Street

  7. Al Zaffat Street

  8. Al Reem Street

  9. Al Maryah Street

  10. Al Rami Street 1

  11. Hamouda Bin Ali Al Dhaheri Street

  12. Electra Park

  13. Al Khabiyah Street

  14. Fatima Bint Mubarak Street

  15. Al Thughour Street

  16. Sultan Bin Zayed The First Street

  17. Al Muna School

  18. Sheikh Rashid Bin Saeed Street

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Abu Dhabi Cultural Foundation

  2. Al Hisn Street

  3. Jaber Al Hindi Mosque

  4. Mubarak Bin Mohammed Street

  5. Dana Plaza

  6. Al Bateen Street

  7. Al Khalidiya Ladies Park

  8. Salam Meizr Mosque

  9. Zayed The First Street

  10. ADNOC HQ

  11. Al Marsa Street

  12. Marina Mall

Other bus routes in Abu Dhabi that you can explore include the 94 bus route, which runs from Wahat Al Karamah to Mamsha Al Saadiyat.

Also See: Abu Dhabi Bus 300: Complete Route and Stop Details

Bus Ticket Price

The Abu Dhabi 67 bus fare is likely around AED 4, which is typical for standard routes. There may be slight variations based on distance. For the most accurate price, use a Hafilat Smart Card (info on the Department of Transport website) or ask the bus driver upon boarding.

Bus Booking

Journey Experience

Taking bus route 67 from Al Reem Shams to Marina Mall offers a delightful journey through Abu Dhabi, starting at the picturesque Al Reem Island, known for its stunning waterfront views and modern architecture.

The route showcases the vibrant lifestyle with bustling streets filled with cafes and shops, as well as serene parks providing a refreshing escape. This bus ride not only takes you to your destination but also serves as a charming tour of Abu Dhabi’s cherished attractions.

Read More: Dubai Bus 81: Comprehensive Guide to Route and Stops

Traveling with Careem

With the Careem app, travelers can conveniently reach Marina Mall, the terminal point on the Bus 67 route originating from Al Reem Shams. This service offers a cost-effective transportation option with fares ranging between AED 46 and 55, making it accessible for residents and visitors alike seeking to explore or commute within Abu Dhabi. Marina Mall’s strategic location ensures easy access to shopping, dining, and leisure amenities, further enhancing the convenience provided by Bus 67.

With its extensive schedule from the ABC departure point, passengers enjoy convenient access to diverse city destinations, ensuring seamless travel experiences throughout the day.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: September 18, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
